channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: ‘Good Omens’ Season 3 Is Still Just One Episode, Despite Being Called That
item.description: The Prime Video series returns May 13 for a 'new season' that's really a movie-length single episode.

item.title: Sony is reportedly testing a subtle PS5 UI update — 'Welcome back PS4'
item.description: Sony is apparently testing a new PS5 UI update.

item.title: Anthropic Supply-Chain Risk Label Should Stay in Place, Appeals Court Says
item.description: The AI company now faces conflicting rulings in its fight over how Claude can be used by the US military.

item.title: Claude, OpenClaw and the new reality: AI agents are here — and so is the chaos
item.description: The age of agentic AI is upon us — whether we like it or not. What started with an innocent question-answer banter with ChatGPT back in 2022 has become an existential debate on job security and the rise of the machines. More recently, fears of reaching artificial general intelligence (AGI) have become more real with the advent of powerful autonomous agents like Claude Cowork and OpenClaw. Having played with these tools for some time, here is a comparison.First, we have OpenClaw (formerly known as Moltbot and Clawdbot). Surpassing 150,000 GitHub stars in days, OpenClaw is already being deploye...

item.title: Could Google Gemini surpass ChatGPT as the biggest AI chatbot within a year? If it maintains its current growth trajectory, I wouldn't bet against it
item.description: Google's chatbot nearly quadrupled market share in twelve months while ChatGPT cooled slowly, setting up a potential leadership battle by 2027.

item.title: Steam Gaming Has Arrived on Apple Vision Pro, and It’s Unexpectedly Glorious
item.description: Valve's Steam Link app is the easiest way to mirror your PC on Vision Pro. Yes, really.

item.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
item.description: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software updates to users.

item.title: RFK Jr. Will Take on Joe Rogan for Podcaster Supremacy
item.description: "This isn't going to be about politics," Kennedy insisted about his new podcast.

item.title: The Spymaster’s Guide to the Secret Agencies of ‘Star Trek’
item.description: The world of 'Star Trek' has always strived to depict a brighter world, but that doesn't mean its galaxy isn't filled with players operating in its shadows.

item.title: Meze Audio's new closed-back headphones are gloriously green and expansive, but the soundstage isn't the only thing about them that's wide
item.description: In which Meze Audio takes elements of one of its more expensive models, adds elements from one of its similarly priced models, but manages to comfortably avoid a Frankenstein’s monster -style outcome…

item.title: Uber Is Testing Driverless Volkswagen ID. Buzz Vans in Los Angeles
item.description: The ride-hailing company plans to start offering public rides by late 2026.

item.title: A self-driving car in Texas hit and killed a mother duck, sparking neighborhood outrage
item.description: An Avride autonomous vehicle near Austin hit the duck, facing backlash from the community. "It didn't slow down or hesitate at all, just steamrolled right through," according to a witness.

item.title: 'Has the Wi-Fi industry been solving the wrong problem?' A team of former Bell Labs and Nokia engineers wants to make your router 10x better thanks to a unique chip
item.description: WavKong introduces a router using Digital Pre-Distortion to improve Wi-Fi consistency, questioning whether industry focus on peak speeds addresses real-world performance.

item.title: Cast Adrift, Meta Employees Have No Idea Who the ‘Token Legend’ Is Anymore
item.description: One week, you're the "Cache Wizard." The next, you're just some poor schmuck.

item.title: AI agent Poke makes setting up automations as easy as sending a text
item.description: Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

item.title: You Can Add ‘Lamp That Folds Your Laundry’ to the List of Doomed Startups
item.description: Finally a $1,500 robotic lamp that folds your shirts.

item.title: Canva doubles down on AI and marketing automation with Simtheory, Ortto acquisitions
item.description: Canva says the acquisitions add strengths in agentic AI, data infrastructure, marketing automation, and customer engagement.
